user_query:
We're a small coworking space in Bogotá planning to buy 12 used Lenovo ThinkPad T480/T490 laptops (16GB RAM, 512GB SSD) for staff and check-in kiosks. Budget cap is COP 18,000,000 total. Timeline: we can wait up to 3 weeks. Quality tolerance: used but reliable with good seller history. Read these files from input/:
- input/listings.jsonl (candidate listings with seller info)
- input/recent_sold_prices.csv (recent sold prices for similar models)
- input/financial_options.csv (Colombian banks' financing terms)
- input/marketing-context.md (tone and audience context)
Then produce these outputs under output/:
1) output/price_comparison.csv — Compare all listings. Columns (exact, in this order): id,title,model,ram_gb,storage_gb,condition,price_cop,seller_rating,link,market_median_cop,market_value_diff_percent. Compute market_median_cop as the median of price_cop from input/recent_sold_prices.csv. Compute market_value_diff_percent per row as 100*(price_cop - market_median_cop)/market_median_cop, rounded to 1 decimal.
2) output/decision.json — A Buy/Wait/Walk decision with structured fields: recommendation ("buy" | "wait" | "walk"), reasoning_summary, budget_cap_cop (should be 18000000), timeline_days (<= 21), units (12), market_median_cop (must match the CSV median), avg_listing_price_cop, priced_vs_median_percent, red_flags (array of strings), scam_risk ("low" | "medium" | "high"), walk_away_unit_price_cop.
3) output/negotiation_scripts.md — Seller outreach and bargaining scripts tailored to our case. Use "Bottom Line → What → Why → How to Act" with confidence tags. Include at least 3 named psychology principles woven into the scripts (e.g., Anchoring, Loss Aversion, Social Proof, Scarcity, Reciprocity). Provide scripts for contacting at least 3 distinct sellers using anchoring first, the flinch, trade-don't-cave, higher authority, and a genuine walk-away.
4) output/financing_plan.json — Compute financing for 50% of the expected total purchase cost based on the average listing price you derive and 12 units. For each row in input/financial_options.csv, include: bank, eligible (boolean based on any min_amount rules present), financed_amount_cop, term_months, monthly_rate_percent, monthly_payment_cop, total_interest_cop, total_pay_cop, notes. Use standard amortization: monthly_payment = A * r / (1 - (1 + r)^(-term_months)), where A = financed_amount_cop and r = monthly_rate_percent/100.
5) output/research_notes.md — Your notes: red flag scan (payment methods, seller behavior, price far below market), marketplace timing, quality risks, and any deal-killer conditions. Keep currency consistently in COP.
6) output/loop/history.json — Define and log an iterative negotiation plan. Structure: { task, criteria, max_iterations, iterations: [ { attempt, action, result, next, timestamp }... ], status }. Criteria: "Target unit price <= 1,300,000 COP and at least 12 units available". Set max_iterations to 5. Log at least 3 attempts (no more than 5). Status must be "complete" or "stopped".
Rules:
- Use recent sold prices (not listings) for market value. Compute and carry through the median to all relevant outputs.
- Use COP everywhere.
- Detect and list scam red flags (e.g., price far below market, wire/crypto/gift cards only, avoidance of written communication, unknown third-party storefronts).
